description: The Texas Syndicate is a mostly Texas-based street and prison gang with predominantly Mexican American membership, founded in 1978 at Folsom State Prison in California. It was established for self-protection against other prison gangs and later engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, prostitution, gambling, and contract murder. The gang has approximately 1,300 members and maintains a paramilitary structure. It operates primarily in Texas but also in prisons across the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Gang members smuggle multikilogram-quantities of powdered cocaine, heroin, and methamphetamine, and multiton-quantities of marijuana from Mexico into the U.S. for distribution inside and outside prison.
country_links.context: The Texas Syndicate smuggles drugs from Mexico into the U.S. and has a direct working relationship with the Gulf Cartel.
